title: wolftpm-tss Error::Transport wraps raw i32 code; no equivalent to wolftpm's TpmRc newtype
description: wolftpm::Error uses TpmRc(u32) — a newtype with is_success(), raw(), Display — to wrap TPM return codes. wolftpm_tss::Error::Transport { code: i32 } uses a raw int. This inconsistency means TSS consumers get a raw i32 they must interpret, while wolftpm consumers get a typed, displayable TpmRc. A TssRc or TransportCode newtype in wolftpm-tss would be consistent with the wolftpm design and allow adding helper methods without a breaking change.
